_maxDynamicPropsComment: PHONE-SAFE dynamic-body budget. Everything-is-physics (2026-07-16) made the built-ins real dynamic bodies, so the restaurant has ~239 pre-removal / ~180 post-removal should-be-dynamic candidates. The referee sorts them GLOBALLY biggest-first; physics._buildProps gives the first `maxDynamicProps` a real rigid body and the rest degrade to a still-collidable but IMMOVABLE static collider. PHYSICS SETTLE ROUND 5 (2026-07-17, VRmike): the old 150 cap left the ~89 SMALLEST candidates (all 16 ketchup + 14 mustard sauce bottles, 13 beige canisters, 2 paper-towel rolls, 20 stools, plates + food scraps) as static colliders that REFUSE TO FALL EVEN WHEN HIT — exactly VRmike's 'the cylinders outright refuse' bug. VRmike's standing rule is everything non-architecture is dynamic and falls, so the cap is raised to 260 (> the 248 promoted / 239 dynamic candidates) so NOTHING a player can knock over is silently demoted to immovable. tools/check-settle.mjs FAILS the build if any should-be-dynamic prop is left without a dynamic body. Correctness over the match-start settle hitch (VRmike pre-approved it). Aggressive Rapier auto-sleep keeps steady-state cost near zero once settled. PHONE HOSTS are the watch item — HOT-TUNABLE (no rebuild): if a warm phone host chugs too hard at match start, lower it (accepting the smallest scraps become unshovable again) or bake pre-settled maps. See notes/physics.md + notes/map-randomization.md.
_pinClutterRemoved: FLOATING-FIXED-PROPS ROUND 4 (2026-07-17, VRmike): the `pinClutterAboveY` pin is GONE. It froze every surface prop authored above 0.5m (plates/food/dishes/condiments) as a FIXED collider hanging at its authored height — you could stand on them and they jittered nearby dynamic bodies. VRmike's standing instruction is EVERYTHING non-architecture is dynamic and falls; only wall/architecture-attached pieces (doors/vents/pillars, flagged `wallAttached`) stay fixed. The launch-out-of-a-tall-hull problem the pin worked around is solved at the source: shared/grounding.js seatMapData seats each item on the collider actually beneath it at load, so nothing spawns interpenetrating. tools/check-floating-props.mjs FAILS the build if a pin (or any non-architecture fixed body) ever comes back.
_mapRandomizeSkipComment: HIDE-SPOT REMOVAL (2026-07-16, VRmike): fraction of DISGUISABLE scenery deleted at match load to open hiding gaps for prop players. Bumped 0.20 -> 0.25, and (this build) widened to cover EVERYTHING a prop can disguise as — the disguise-pool props (map.props) AND every non-architecture fixture (knockable tables/cookware AND bolted-in built-ins: counters, pillars, fridge, doors). Architecture (floors/walls/ceilings) is never eligible. Same rule (physics.isDisguisableEntry) drives removal and the disguise pool so they can't drift. See referee.startMatch + notes/map-randomization.md.
_shotImpulseComment: SHOT IMPULSE (2026-07-12, VRmike): a shot that lands on a DYNAMIC (knockable) prop gives it a small host-authoritative physics kick at the hit point along the shot direction, so shot items visibly react. This is the TARGET linear speed kick in m/s — shared/physics.js scales it by the hit body's mass so a heavy table and a light burger both get the SAME visible nudge (not a mass-based launch of the tiny props). Keep it small (a nudge, not a rocket launcher); raise for a punchier feel. HOT-TUNABLE, no rebuild. Host-only; replicates through the normal prop snapshot stream.
_finderComment: PROP FINDER — the hunter's second selectable tool (2026-07-17, VRmike). When selected it renders a large TRANSLUCENT CYLINDER (green ready / grey cooling) centred on the hunter; LEFT-CLICK (PC) / the fire button (mobile) activates it, forcing a random UNCANCELLABLE taunt out of EVERY living prop within finderRadius (2D distance — the cylinder is effectively infinite in height, so height is ignored). finderRadius is METRES; finderCooldownSeconds is a PER-HUNTER, host-enforced cooldown (each hunter counts down independently, never shared). Both are HOT-TUNABLE (no rebuild) — VRmike expects to adjust them in testing. See shared/referee.js applyFind + notes/prop-finder.md.
_fireRateComment: RAPID FIRE (2026-07-12, VRmike): the assault rifle is now HOLD-to-fire at a realistic assault-rifle/SMG cadence. fireRateRpm is the single tunable (rounds per minute; 600-800 is the real-world band). The HOST derives its authoritative fire-rate cap from this (60000/rpm ms between shots, minus a small jitter grace so a legit on-time client isn't throttled below the intended rate) so a modified client can't shoot faster; the CLIENT paces its held-fire auto-repeat off the same number. Damage per bullet is UNCHANGED (damage.base = 5%). fireCooldownMs above is the legacy fallback used only if fireRateRpm is absent.
_damageComment: HUNTER-TOOLS v1 damage tuning (all hot-tunable, no rebuild). base = damage a hunter's rifle does to an undisguised player (5 => 20 hits to kill at defaultMult; DAMAGE-TUNING 2026-07-12 lowered this 10->5). A disguised prop-PLAYER scales base by a SIZE multiplier lerped between smallSize/largeSize (metres, = 2*max half-extent of the disguise's footprint — the SAME size source physics bakes colliders from, so it auto-upgrades to measured bounds). smallMult (tiny, e.g. burger ~0.72 m => still dies in ~2 hits at 50 dmg — rescaled 5->10 to hold ~2 hits at the new 5 base) lerps down to largeMult (big, e.g. table ~2.25 m => ~3x the default player's bullets at ~1.7 dmg => ~59 hits vs 20). Sizes at/below smallSize clamp to smallMult; at/above largeSize clamp to largeMult. defaultMult applies to an UNDISGUISED player (no size). WRONG-GUESS PENALTY (a hunter shooting a disguisable decoy — a prop or non-arch fixture that COULD have been a player) is now a FLAT `base` hit with NO size multiplier EVER (20 wrong guesses = dead); real architecture is still a free miss. selfScalesWithSize is retained as false and is NO LONGER read by the referee (the penalty is unconditionally flat).
_grenadeComment: HUNTER GRENADES — the hunter's THIRD selectable tool (2026-07-17, VRmike). LEFT-CLICK (PC) / the fire button (mobile) while the grenade is selected fires a RAYCAST from the aim point into the world; the grenade explodes INSTANTLY at the first hit point (no arc/travel/fuse/cooldown). All three knobs are HOT-TUNABLE (no rebuild). baseDamage is a FRACTION of full health (0.45 = 45%); the referee multiplies it by startHealth to get HP, then by the SAME prop-size multiplier the rifle uses (so tiny burger props take proportionally more and die — the whole point). RADIUS + FALLOFF are authored as 1 + 2, NOT as an outer radius of 3, exactly as VRmike asked (less error-prone to edit): fullDamageRadius = the metres of MAX damage; falloffDistance = the metres ADDED past it over which damage lerps to ~0, so total range = fullDamageRadius + falloffDistance = 1 + 2 = 3. d<=1m => full (base×sizeMult); 1m<d<3m => base×sizeMult×(1-(d-1)/2); d>=3m => 0. BACKFIRE: the blast also hurts the THROWING hunter through non-player decoy props (the same 'could be a player but isn't' objects the rifle backfires on) — flat baseDamage×falloff, NO size multiplier, so ~3 direct decoy hits (0.45×3=1.35 of full HP) are lethal WITHOUT hardcoding '3'. NO friendly fire, NO direct self-damage. REDEMPTION: if the blast kills >=1 prop PLAYER the thrower is restored to FULL HP even when backfire would have been lethal (host resolves prop-player damage first, then decides backfire). Host-authoritative — the client only sends its aim origin/direction; the host recomputes the blast point. See shared/damage.js grenadeFalloff + shared/referee.js applyGrenade + notes/hunter-grenades.md.
